title: polypodium rheedei
intro: Polypodium rheedei, also known as Rheede's polypody, is a species of fern native to the Western Ghats of India. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The health benefits of Polypodium rheedei include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating respiratory and gastrointestinal issues, as well as skin conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing it in water or oil, and it is often used in the form of decoctions or tinctures to harness its medicinal benefits.
